gloss.en: in order to help someone

words.example: She spoke on behalf of the workers.

gloss.en: loved very much

words.example: He lost his beloved wife last year.

gloss.en: a long wooden seat for several people

words.example: We sat on a park bench.

gloss.en: a standard used for comparison

words.example: This test sets a benchmark for the others.

gloss.en: in a lower position than; under

words.example: They found the body beneath a pile of leaves.

gloss.en: a person who gains from something

words.example: She is the main beneficiary of the new law.

gloss.en: to give secrets to an enemy; be disloyal

words.example: He was paid to betray his colleagues.

gloss.en: to tie things together with rope or string

words.example: They used rope to bind his hands.

gloss.en: the story of a person's life by someone else

words.example: She wrote a biography of the famous king.

gloss.en: a senior priest in charge of a district

words.example: The bishop spoke at the church.

gloss.en: very strange or unusual

words.example: It was a bizarre situation.

gloss.en: the sharp flat cutting part of a knife or tool

words.example: The knife has a sharp blade.

gloss.en: an explosion or powerful rush of air

words.example: The bomb blast destroyed the building.

gloss.en: to lose blood from a wound

words.example: My finger started to bleed.

gloss.en: a mixture of different types of one thing

words.example: This is a special blend of tea.

gloss.en: to ask God to protect someone

words.example: The priest came to bless the children.

gloss.en: God's help and protection; a prayer for this

words.example: They prayed for God's blessing.

gloss.en: to talk too proudly about what you have

words.example: I don't want to boast about my success.

gloss.en: extra money added to your pay as a reward

words.example: She got a Christmas bonus this year.

gloss.en: a sudden increase in trade and success

words.example: Living standards rose during the economic boom.

gloss.en: to move quickly back after hitting a surface

words.example: Watch the ball bounce off the wall.

gloss.en: a line that marks the edge of something

words.example: The river forms the boundary between the two countries.

gloss.en: the act of bending your head to show respect

words.example: She gave a slight bow of her head.

gloss.en: a failure to obey a law or rule

words.example: This is a breach of contract.

gloss.en: when a vehicle or machine stops working

words.example: We had a breakdown on the motorway.

gloss.en: an important new development or success

words.example: Scientists made a major breakthrough this year.

gloss.en: a particular type of animal

words.example: Labradors are a popular breed of dog.

gloss.en: a fast, always-on way to connect to the internet

words.example: They plan to bring fast broadband to rural areas.

gloss.en: a program for reading web pages

words.example: What do you use as your default browser?

gloss.en: violent and cruel

words.example: It was a brutal attack.

gloss.en: a US or Australian dollar (informal)

words.example: They cost ten bucks.

gloss.en: a friend

words.example: He is an old college buddy of mine.

gloss.en: a thing that reduces a shock or protects

words.example: Savings act as a buffer against hard times.

gloss.en: the main part; most of something

words.example: The bulk of the population lives in cities.

gloss.en: a heavy duty that causes worry or hard work

words.example: He had to carry the burden alone.

gloss.en: complicated official rules and procedures

words.example: There is too much bureaucracy in government.

gloss.en: the act of burying a dead body

words.example: The burial took place at dawn.

gloss.en: to break open suddenly from inside pressure

words.example: That balloon will burst if you blow it more.

gloss.en: a group of senior government ministers

words.example: The cabinet met to discuss the new policy.

gloss.en: the process of finding an amount using numbers

words.example: Cathy did a rough calculation.

gloss.en: strong cloth used for tents or for painting on

words.example: The tents were made from heavy canvas.

gloss.en: the ability or skill needed to do something

words.example: Age affects a person's capability to learn.

gloss.en: an economy run for profit by private owners

words.example: They studied the growth of industrial capitalism.

gloss.en: based on the ideas of capitalism

words.example: They live in a capitalist society.

gloss.en: goods carried in a ship or plane

words.example: The tanker spilled its cargo of oil.

gloss.en: a section of a train for passengers

words.example: We found seats in the first carriage.

gloss.en: to make shapes by cutting wood or stone

words.example: He likes to carve animals out of wood.

gloss.en: a building where people gamble for money

words.example: They opened a new casino in Las Vegas.

gloss.en: a person killed or injured in war or an accident

words.example: Our goal is to reduce road casualties.

gloss.en: a complete list of items to view or buy

words.example: She ordered the dress from a catalogue.

gloss.en: to provide food and drink for an event

words.example: Our company can cater for your wedding.

gloss.en: cows and bulls kept on a farm

words.example: A herd of cattle grazed in the field.

gloss.en: care taken to avoid danger or mistakes

words.example: Please drive with great caution.

gloss.en: careful to avoid danger or mistakes

words.example: The government was cautious in its response.

gloss.en: to stop happening or existing

words.example: The payments cease when you start a job.

gloss.en: an area of land for burying the dead

words.example: He was buried in a private cemetery.

gloss.en: a hall used for formal meetings

words.example: The members left the council chamber.

gloss.en: a complete lack of order

words.example: The strike caused economic chaos.

gloss.en: to be typical of a person, place or thing

words.example: Rolling hills characterize this part of England.

gloss.en: the power of pleasing or attracting people

words.example: He was a man of great charm.

gloss.en: a written statement of a group's rights

words.example: They signed a charter of workers' rights.

gloss.en: lasting a long time; hard to cure

words.example: She suffers from chronic asthma.

gloss.en: a thick solid piece cut from something

words.example: He cut a big chunk of cheese.

gloss.en: to move continuously around a place or system

words.example: Exercise helps the blood circulate freely.

gloss.en: the movement of blood around the body

words.example: Regular exercise improves blood circulation.

gloss.en: the legal right to belong to a country

words.example: They were granted full French citizenship.

gloss.en: officially connected with a town or city

words.example: The new civic buildings opened today.

gloss.en: connected with people not in the armed forces

words.example: He left the army and returned to civilian life.

gloss.en: the quality of being expressed clearly

words.example: There is a lack of clarity in the law.

gloss.en: a short fight between two groups

words.example: A clash broke out between police and protesters.

gloss.en: the act of putting things into groups

words.example: This music defies easy classification.

gloss.en: to hold on tightly to something

words.example: The child clung to her mother's hand.

gloss.en: relating to treating patients and illnesses

words.example: The new drug is in clinical trials.

gloss.en: the permanent shutting of a factory or school

words.example: The factory closure cost many jobs.

gloss.en: a group of similar things close together

words.example: The telescope showed a dense cluster of stars.

gloss.en: a government formed by two or more parties

words.example: The two parties formed a coalition.

gloss.en: of or near a coast

words.example: They sailed through coastal waters.

gloss.en: a drink mixing spirits and fruit juice

words.example: She ordered a cocktail at the bar.

gloss.en: connected with mental processes of understanding

words.example: They studied the child's cognitive development.

gloss.en: to happen at the same time

words.example: Our trips to New York don't coincide.

gloss.en: to work together to produce something

words.example: Researchers collaborate to develop a new vaccine.

gloss.en: the act of working together to create something

words.example: The book was a collaboration between two writers.

gloss.en: shared by all members of a group

words.example: They share collective responsibility for the result.

gloss.en: an accident where two things crash together

words.example: There was a collision between two trains.

gloss.en: connected with a country that controls another

words.example: Britain was once a great colonial power.

gloss.en: a journalist who writes regular articles

words.example: She works as a newspaper columnist.

gloss.en: fighting, especially during a war

words.example: He was killed in combat.

gloss.en: to begin to happen; to begin something

words.example: The meeting will commence at noon.

gloss.en: a spoken description of an event as it happens

words.example: We listened to the sports commentary.

gloss.en: a person who describes an event on TV or radio

words.example: He works as a sports commentator.

gloss.en: trade; the buying and selling of goods

words.example: Leaders of industry and commerce met in Paris.

gloss.en: a member of an official controlling group

words.example: The police commissioner gave a statement.

gloss.en: a product or raw material bought and sold

words.example: Rice and flour are basic commodities.

gloss.en: connected with communism

words.example: They studied communist ideology.

gloss.en: a person or animal you spend time with

words.example: My dog is a loyal companion.

gloss.en: similar and able to be compared

words.example: A comparable house would cost twice as much.

gloss.en: sympathy for those suffering and wish to help

words.example: She showed great compassion for the poor.

gloss.en: to force someone to do something

words.example: The law can compel fathers to pay.

gloss.en: so interesting that it holds your attention
gloss.zh: 引人入胜的；令人信服的

words.example: Her latest book makes compelling reading.

gloss.en: to balance or reduce the bad effects of loss

words.example: Nothing can compensate for the loss of a friend.

gloss.en: money given to make up for harm or loss

words.example: She received compensation for the injury.

gloss.en: the ability to do something well

words.example: He gained a high level of competence in English.

gloss.en: having enough skill to do something well

words.example: He is very competent in his work.

gloss.en: to produce a book or list by collecting items

words.example: We are trying to compile a list of names.

gloss.en: to add to something in a way that improves it

words.example: A good wine list complements the menu.

gloss.en: the state of being complicated or detailed

words.example: They study the complexity of modern systems.

gloss.en: the act of obeying rules or authority

words.example: These steps ensure full compliance with the law.

gloss.en: something that makes a situation more difficult

words.example: The bad weather added a further complication.

gloss.en: to obey a rule or order

words.example: They refused to comply with the new rules.

gloss.en: the parts that something is made of

words.example: They tested the chemical composition of the soil.

gloss.en: a deal where each side gives up something

words.example: After long talks they reached a compromise.

gloss.en: to calculate something

words.example: The losses were computed at five million pounds.

gloss.en: to hide someone or something

words.example: He tried to conceal his true feelings.

gloss.en: to admit something is true after resisting

words.example: He had to concede that she was right.

gloss.en: to form an idea or plan in your mind

words.example: He conceived the idea of a new arts centre.

gloss.en: an understanding or belief about something

words.example: They debated his conception of social justice.

gloss.en: something you give up to end an argument

words.example: The firm was forced to make concessions.

gloss.en: to say strongly that something is very bad

words.example: The government condemned the killings.

gloss.en: to discuss something to get advice or opinions

words.example: He wanted to confer with his colleagues first.

gloss.en: a statement admitting you are guilty

words.example: After hours of questioning, she made a full confession.
